zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Burgh於1133年4月2日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆時，他的父親William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland25歲，母親Juliana Daisnel21歲。他於大約1157年與Alice de Bourgogne在英國諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。他去世於1179年7月9日在他的家鄉享年46歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter de Burgh于1133年4月2日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆时，他的父亲William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland25岁，母亲Juliana Daisnel21岁。他于大约1157年与Alice de Bourgogne在英国諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆结婚。他们至少是8个儿子的父母。他去世于1179年7月9日在他的家乡享年46岁，埋葬在爱尔兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Burgh was born on 2 April 1133, in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland, was 25 and his mother, Juliana Daisnel, was 21. He married Alice de Bourgogne about 1157, in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. He died on 9 July 1179, in his hometown, at the age of 46, and was buried in Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter de Burgh est né le 2 avril 1133, à Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Juliana Daisnel, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Bourgogne environ 1157, à Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Il est décédé le 9 juillet 1179, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Irlande.

April 1133 in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland 25 und seine Mutter Juliana Daisnel 21. Er hat um 1157 in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England Alice de Bourgogne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Er starb am 9. Juli 1179 im Alter von 46 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Irland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter de Burghが1133年4月2日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャムで生まれた時，父William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Irelandは25歳，母Juliana Daisnelは21歳であった。約1157年にイギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャムでAlice de Bourgogneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼は1179年7月9日に故郷で46歳で死去し，アイルランドにて埋葬された。

ko.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤
ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Burgh(이)가 1133년 4월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Juliana Daisnel(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1157년 영국 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤에서 Alice de Bourgogne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1179년 7월 9일 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Burgh nasceu em 2 de abril de 1133, em Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Juliana Daisnel, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Bourgogne aproximadamente 1157, em Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos. Ele faleceu em 9 de julho de 1179, em sua cidade natal, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Irlanda.

ru.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter de Burgh родился 2 апреля 1133 г. в местности бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Juliana Daisnel, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Bourgogne около 1157 г. в местности бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Он умер 9 июля 1179 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности Ирландия.
